system.descriptor: Interrogation allows a character to extract information from an unwilling subject through psychology, specialised tools, serums, and other invasive methods.
system.uses: Uses: Opposed test against the target's Willpower. If the character wins, he gets one answer for each degree of success. If the opponent wins, the interrogator gets nothing of worth. A roll with two or more degrees of failure inflicts 1d10 plus the interrogator's Willpower bonus in damage to the subject and prevents further interrogation for 1d5 days. A roll with four or more degrees of failure also gives the subject +30 to resist further Interrogation by the character or his allies. Each Interrogation test inflicts one level of Fatigue on the target.

description.value: Interrogation allows a character to extract information from an unwilling subject through psychology, specialised tools, serums, and other invasive methods.
